entities.name: Seal of God
entities.raw_quote: Besides examples on metal, it can be found in manuscripts and at least one printed edition of the Key of Solomon.

entities.name: Hocrohel

attributes.action: called the text consecrated before the Lord

entities.raw_quote: because the angel Hocrohel called it consecrated before the Lord.

entities.name: Solomon
entities.raw_quote: stating the writings of Honorius were “second only to those of Solomon in notoriety in the medi-eval period.

entities.name: Seal of Solomon
entities.raw_quote: The great seal is called the Seal of Solomon (as well as the Seal of God), famous from antiquity for controlling demons.

entities.name: Theban alphabet
entities.raw_quote: This so-called Theban alphabet is clearly based on the Latin alphabet, not Greek.

entities.name: beatific vision
entities.raw_quote: The centerpiece of the text is the ritual to attain nothing less than the beatific vision.

entities.name: Layaly

attributes.context: part of seven divine names in Seal of God

entities.raw_quote: seven divine names used in the Seal of God (IV.14–47, CXXVII.15): Layaly, etc.

entities.name: magic circle
entities.raw_quote: might these have inspired Honorius to design his magic circle with a sword for each planet, stationed at seven directions around the perimeter?
